page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.large is an ARM64-based virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores on an Ampere Altra processor running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1824.28. However, its multi-core and memory subsystem capabilities are more constrained. Multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng is rated as weak at 3617.67, and memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor results, including a read-only large block bandwidth of 26.08 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 16.11 GB/sec. Passmark testing also reflects these limitations, with a CPU score of 1594.77 and a memory score of 1056.07, both falling into the lower performance tiers.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.c6r.large is its modest memory capacity and constrained memory bandwidth, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications or high-throughput databases. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated ARM64 architecture offers a predictable compute profile for lightweight, multi-threaded workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic Redis key-value operations, simple single-threaded file compression tasks, and low-demand application hosting where dedicated CPU allocation is required but memory demands are minimal.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.large is an ARM64-based virtual server instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of memory, powered by a 2.8 GHz Ampere Altra processor under the KVM hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3617.67 and a Passmark memory score of 1056.07. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient, dedicated compute resource for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, simple Redis operations, and low-intensity application hosting that do not require high memory capacity or intensive multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.large is an ARM64 server with 2 dedicated Ampere Altra vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight dedicated compute workloads.

tagline: A dedicated ARM64 virtual server offering balanced single-core performance and low memory footprint for lightweight, predictable compute workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average single-core CPU performance with predictable dedicated core allocation
Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic Redis operations, and simple application hosting
